acceptance_targets: [CH07_CONTINUITY_PHOTO_TIMING] PASS if a cold reader can reconcile the school-year label at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:227, Damian's age marker at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:229, and the elapsed-time language at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:241 without contradiction. FAIL if the passage still presents Damian as about ten in the photo while placing it sixteen years before the present.

[CH07_GROSZ_BRIEFING_HANDOUT] PASS if revised snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:149 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:205 contain 1) one concrete lead Damian can now pursue on the page-level terms of the chapter, such as a name, intermediary, document type, venue, or contract trail, 2) one visible cost or risk created by the conversation, and 3) at least one major uncertainty that remains explicitly uncertain. FAIL if Grosz still delivers the chapter's full geopolitical model as mostly uninterrupted answers to Damian's prompts.

[CH07_COMPOSED_DIALOGUE_REGISTER] PASS if the revised lunch exchange at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:73 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:91 and the revised Grosz exchange at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:165 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:205 each contain at least one pressure-disrupted line per speaker, such as an interruption, self-correction, unfinished sentence, terse refusal, or abrupt redirect, and no stretch of three consecutive explanatory mini-speeches without a leverage shift or concrete interruption. FAIL if Demetrius and Grosz still sound like adjacent versions of the same polished explainer voice.
[RED-031] Ch 7 em-dash-containing prose lines are at or below 8%.

[LW03-001] A consecutive read from chapter 7 into chapter 8 should leave no ambiguity about Damian's state: if he has already named Anya and linked her to the attache in chapter 7, chapter 8 must show explicit suspicion or altered conduct; if chapter 8 still needs the near-recognition beat, chapter 7 must stop short of the name.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if a cold reader can reconcile the school-year label at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:227, Damian's age marker at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:229, and the elapsed-time language at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:241 without contradiction. FAIL if the passage still presents Damian as about ten in the photo while placing it sixteen years before the present.
findings.problem: The photo passage breaks canonical chronology in its own lines. The image is framed as an Eden Academy third-year class photo, Damian is described as ten in that photo, and then the narration calls the moment "sixteen years ago." With Damian established as twenty in the continuity sheet, that math is impossible and disrupts the recognition beat instead of supporting it.
findings.rewrite_direction: Correct the age math in the photo passage at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:227, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:229, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:241. Either keep the third-year/age-ten framing and change the elapsed-time reference to an age-consistent interval, or deliberately change the age markers in the same local passage so the chronology reconciles cleanly.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:149 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:205 contain 1) one concrete lead Damian can now pursue on the page-level terms of the chapter, such as a name, intermediary, document type, venue, or contract trail, 2) one visible cost or risk created by the conversation, and 3) at least one major uncertainty that remains explicitly uncertain. FAIL if Grosz still delivers the chapter's full geopolitical model as mostly uninterrupted answers to Damian's prompts.
findings.problem: The chapter's main political turn arrives as a low-friction debrief. Damian asks about the Renewal Front, and Grosz supplies coalition structure, funding mechanism, leadership psychology, bilateral profiteering, and the strategic warning in tidy escalating answers. Because the information arrives with almost no contest, proof, or cost, the scene reads more like a handoff of authorial explanation than a dangerous intelligence exchange.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild the Grosz scene from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:149 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:205 so Damian has to earn the intelligence. Give him a concrete datum to bring, force Grosz to test or refuse him, and make at least one major claim arrive through contested inference, partial documentation, or a risky ask rather than complete summary. The scene should end with a new operational burden, not just a cleaner theory.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised lunch exchange at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:73 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:91 and the revised Grosz exchange at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:165 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:205 each contain at least one pressure-disrupted line per speaker, such as an interruption, self-correction, unfinished sentence, terse refusal, or abrupt redirect, and no stretch of three consecutive explanatory mini-speeches without a leverage shift or concrete interruption. FAIL if Demetrius and Grosz still sound like adjacent versions of the same polished explainer voice.
findings.problem: The chapter's dominant dialogue register is over-composed. Demetrius and Grosz both speak in balanced thesis lines, aphoristic closures, and mini-speech diagnoses, which makes the lunch and apartment scenes sound written-about rather than overheard. The polish also flattens idiolect separation: a ruthless older brother and a frightened retired operative should not keep landing in the same essay-ready cadence.
findings.rewrite_direction: Roughen and differentiate the exchanges at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:73, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:83, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:87, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:91 and at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:165, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:175, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:183, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:191, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:201, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:205. Keep Demetrius managerial and controlled, but let sibling shorthand, impatience, and interruption do more work; keep Grosz articulate, but make his caution audible through hedges, refusals, broken starts, and operationally specific fragments instead of mini-lectures.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 7 em-dash-containing prose lines are at or below 8%.
findings.evidence: Ch 7: 35 of 119 prose lines (29.4%) contain em-dashes.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce em-dash-containing lines in Ch 7 from 35 to ~10 (target ~8%).

findings.acceptance_test: A consecutive read from chapter 7 into chapter 8 should leave no ambiguity about Damian's state: if he has already named Anya and linked her to the attache in chapter 7, chapter 8 must show explicit suspicion or altered conduct; if chapter 8 still needs the near-recognition beat, chapter 7 must stop short of the name.

evidence.excerpt: 1933: Anya Forger. The girl who'd punched him during a dodgeball game in year one because he'd said something about her hair and she'd decided the appropriate response was violence. The girl who'd ended up on his cleanup crew at the school festival and spent the entire afternoon arguing about the correct way to stack chairs. The girl who'd been the first person at Eden Academy to treat him like a person instead of a surname, which at the time had enraged him because he'd built his entire identity around being a surname and she'd refused to notice.

1935: He thought about the cultural attache he'd met at a state dinner. The woman with the quick smile and the laugh that caught him somewhere he hadn't expected. The way she'd tilted her head when she was listening — not politely but with a kind of total attention that made him feel like the only frequency in a crowded room.

findings.problem: Chapter 7 lets Damian retrieve 'Anya Forger' from the Eden photo and immediately compare that remembered girl to the cultural attache, but chapter 8 later frames the same recognition as still one synapse short of the name. Read back-to-back, his awareness moves backward at the boundary instead of forward.
findings.rewrite_direction:
Pick one awareness level and carry it cleanly across the boundary. Either pull chapter 7 back to a charged but unresolved photo beat, or let chapter 8 begin from Damian already holding the name and adjusting his behavior around Anya accordingly.

Aggregator guidance:
- Cross-chapter context: Ch 8's reception scene frames Damian's recognition as still one synapse short of the name — he thinks 'pink hair,' 'Eden Academy,' 'school festival' but does not reach the name 'Anya Forger.' If Ch 7 lets Damian retrieve the full name from the Eden photo and connect it to the current cultural attaché, Ch 8's near-miss reads as a backward step. Either pull Ch 7 back to a charged but unresolved photo beat (he stares at a girl with pink hair, doesn't connect her to the woman he's been working with), or Ch 8 must start from Damian already holding the name.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's premise blends comedy, espionage action, and genuine emotional stakes. The creative risk is in honoring all three simultaneously without letting any one mode cannibalize the others. Do not soften the violence because the novel is funny. Do not suppress the comedy because the stakes are real. Do not retreat from Anya's moral complicity (she reads minds without consent and uses it to manipulate) because she is the protagonist and we like her. The hardest scenes to get right are the ones where comedy and pain coexist — a fight that is also funny, a confession that is also absurd, a family dinner where love and deception are the same meal. Lean into those moments rather than resolving them into one register. The novel's best material lives in the dissonance.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism at the point of consequence is a craft failure. When violence, fear, grief, or moral compromise demands scene presence, render it with specificity. A bruise should have a color. A death should have weight. A lie should have a cost the liar feels in their body. The exception is Anya's telepathic restraint — she sometimes cannot bear to fully articulate what she's reading in someone's mind, and this selective omission is a character choice, not authorial squeamishness.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters in casual speech. Uncontracted forms reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific register choices (Sylvia's professional diction, Damian's diplomatic mode). In contemporary casual scenes, an unjustified full-form line should be flagged and checked.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should share the same structural rhythm. Alternate between dialogue-heavy scenes and interiority-heavy passages. Vary scene count: two-scene chapters (11, 16, 17 — compressed force) against multi-scene chapters (8, 15 — breadth and momentum). Shift between comic dominant and dramatic dominant across the middle of the book, with the comic register maintaining presence even in dramatic chapters through Anya's voice and the Forger family's inherent absurdity. The tonal shift at Chapter 13 is a deliberate gear change, not a gradual dimming — comedy should be fully alive in Chapter 12 and markedly reduced (not absent) in Chapter 13. The Chapter 13 POV shift to Damian is a structural event: the reader's relationship to information changes. The final act (14-17) maintains Anya's voice and the family's warmth as comic undertone, but the dominant mode is drama with consequences. Chapter 17's dinner scene reintroduces comedy through a register the novel has not previously accessed — Damian's self-conscious thought management around a known telepath — which only works because it follows sixteen chapters of oblivious thought-reading.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary and accessible, not literary-precious. The prose should have energy and forward drive. Humor comes from observation, character logic, and the gap between surface and interior — not from clever narration or authorial commentary. Let the funny things be funny without signposting them. Let the sad things sit without interpretation. The prose should be invisible when it needs to be and vivid when the scene demands it, but never decorative for its own sake.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Narrator-as-comedian: the prose commenting on its own comedy, winking at the reader, or admiring a character's wit. The humor lives in the situation, not in the telling.

Telepathy-as-omniscience: Anya's mind-reading should feel like cognitive overload, not like a narrator's convenient access to exposition. She reads surface thoughts, not deep motivations, and the thoughts she catches should be fragmentary, mundane, and sometimes misleading.

Prestige-creep: the prose drifting toward solemn literary register when the scene's natural mode is comic, warm, or chaotic. This novel's emotional power comes from the coexistence of comedy and genuine feeling, not from the comedy yielding to seriousness.

Briefing-itis: scenes where characters explain plot to each other or to the reader. Information should emerge through action, observation, and telepathic fragments — not through expository dialogue formatted as conversation.

Combat-poetry: fight scenes that become lyrical or aesthetically admiring. Violence should be fast, specific, physically grounded, and over quickly. Yor's lethality is matter-of-fact; Anya's fighting is scrappier and less elegant.

Emotion-labeling: telling the reader what a character feels instead of rendering the feeling through thought, action, and physical detail. 'She felt sad' is always worse than the specific thing that makes the sadness legible.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, primarily Anya. Chapter 7 (Damian), Chapter 10 (Yor), the Damian POV section in Chapter 13, and Damian's thread in Chapter 15 shift to close third on their respective POV characters. The telepathy layer is unique to Anya's POV — when she is the viewpoint character, other characters' surface thoughts appear as italicized interjections woven into the prose. In non-Anya POV chapters, no telepathic content appears. The Chapter 13 Damian POV section is structurally critical: it gives the reader access to Damian's interior at a depth Anya's telepathy cannot reach, and it reveals that he has identified Anya — knowledge the reader carries into the final act while Anya does not. This dramatic irony reversal is the novel's strongest structural surprise and depends on the reader experiencing Damian's depths unmediated by Anya's telepathic filter.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied and responsive to scene pressure. Action sequences: short sentences, concrete verbs, sensory immediacy. Comic scenes: longer sentences that build to turns, pacing that allows beats to land. Quiet domestic scenes: relaxed rhythm with room for observation and texture. Telepathic interjections should disrupt prose rhythm the way intrusive thoughts disrupt concentration — not ornamentally but as genuine cognitive interference. Avoid metronomic sentence patterns in any mode.
sensory_bias: visual surveillance — reading rooms, reading faces, the spy's hyperawareness of spatial arrangement and exit routes
auditory — overheard thoughts as a telepathic sixth sense layered over actual ambient sound
kinesthetic — action choreography, physical proximity, the body under stress or in intimacy
olfactory/gustatory — Yor's cooking, Berlint street food, the specific smell of institutional buildings and safe houses
